records.ety: [OE. scrippe, probably of Scand. origin; cf. Icel. & OSw. skreppa, and also LL. scrippum, OF. esquerpe, escrepe, F. charpe scarf. Cf. Scrap, Scarf a piece of dress.]

defs: A small bag; a wallet; a satchel.
quotes: And in requital ope his leathern scrip.

defs: A small writing, certificate, or schedule; a piece of paper containing a writing.
quotes: Call them generally, man by man, according to the scrip.
Bills of exchange can not pay our debts abroad, till scrips of paper can be made current coin.

defs: A preliminary certificate of a subscription to the capital of a bank, railroad, or other company, or for a share of other joint property, or a loan, stating the amount of the subscription and the date of the payment of the installments; as, insurance scrip, consol scrip, etc. When all the installments are paid, the scrip is exchanged for a bond share certificate.
